title: Volatility transition asymmetry
plain_language_summary_zh: 研究波动从低位转高时，上下行后的持续性是否存在稳定不对称。
falsifiable_hypothesis: Low-to-high realized-volatility transitions exhibit different fixed-horizon persistence after upward versus downward shocks across both approved Development pairs.
proposed_market_mechanism: Leverage adjustment and liquidation sensitivity may make volatility transitions directionally asymmetric.
supporting_evidence: The frozen scope includes two approved futures Development datasets for cross-pair descriptive comparison.
contradictory_evidence: Observed asymmetry may be fully explained by unequal shock magnitudes.

source_refs.claim: The frozen state authorizes BTC and ETH Development-only descriptive research.

novelty_vs_existing_research: Focuses on transition direction and counterevidence rather than volatility level alone.
minimal_test_method: Bucket fixed low-to-high volatility transitions by shock direction and compare forward persistence after matching shock magnitude bins.
falsification_conditions: Direction-conditioned differences disappear after fixed shock-magnitude matching.
stop_conditions: Stop if fixed bins produce insufficient observations on either approved pair.
known_limitations: Realized-volatility estimates overlap adjacent hourly observations.
